title: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD if it contains libscalene.so and other values
body: It looks like in some cases setup_preload gets called more than once, so it was adding `libscalene.so` to `LD_PRELOAD` the first time then later replacing `LD_PRELOAD`  with just `libscalene.so`, this fixes that case.

step_plan.summary: Modify ScalenePreload.get_preload_environ to explicitly handle three LD_PRELOAD scenarios so existing LD_PRELOAD values that already include 'libscalene.so' are preserved and not overwritten when setup_preload runs multiple times.

steps.operation: Fix LD_PRELOAD construction to preserve existing entries when libscalene.so is already present
steps.reason: The current conditional conflates the cases "LD_PRELOAD absent" and "LD_PRELOAD already contains 'libscalene.so'", causing the latter to be overwritten and losing other libraries. Implement explicit branching for the three cases: LD_PRELOAD absent -> set to 'libscalene.so'; LD_PRELOAD present and missing 'libscalene.so' -> prepend 'libscalene.so:'; LD_PRELOAD present and already containing 'libscalene.so' -> preserve os.environ['LD_PRELOAD'] (do not replace). This prevents corruption when setup_preload is invoked multiple times.
steps.side_effects: Low risk change limited to environment construction for LD_PRELOAD. Tests that previously observed the incorrect overwrite must be updated or new tests added; otherwise behavior is unchanged for first-time setup. Downstream callers (e.g., setup_preload logic that compares requested env to os.environ) will now correctly detect matching values and avoid unnecessary respawns. No external API changes, but any code that implicitly relied on LD_PRELOAD being replaced (instead of preserved) may observe different behavior. Recommend adding unit tests covering: LD_PRELOAD absent, present without libscalene, present with only libscalene, and present with libscalene plus other entries to prevent regressions.
